findings.evidence: Resolved: POST /classes again binds the legacy Name + LecturerId contract and creates a deliberately unassigned legacy Class, while the new admin UI creates subject-linked classes through the additive POST /classes/subject-scoped endpoint. Both frontends compile the enrollment UI behind VITE_COURSE_ENROLLMENT_ENABLED, whose Dockerfile/default behavior is false unless explicitly set to the exact string true. docker-compose opts in only for the coordinated stack and starts each web image after the healthy Gateway, whose own health dependency includes the Catalog API.
findings.why_it_matters: A rolling or independently cached frontend/backend deployment can make class administration or student Profile fail during the compatibility window, violating the phase's mixed-version rollout constraint.
findings.required_action: Introduce an explicit compatibility boundary: preserve/version the legacy class-create contract during the overlap and gate new admin/student UI activation until every serving Catalog instance supports the new endpoints (or document and implement an equivalent atomic deployment mechanism). Keep newly created subject-linked classes mandatory once the new path is enabled.

findings.evidence: Resolved: the Classes navigation item is emitted only when the enrollment feature is enabled and the current role is admin. The /classes route is nested under RequireAdmin, which redirects lecturers and feature-disabled sessions to /dashboard before ClassManagementPage mounts, so lecturer sessions never invoke the admin-only class list.
findings.why_it_matters: A supported lecturer account is led to a visible route that now deterministically fails after this change, creating a role-specific regression unrelated to the intended admin-only mutation controls.
findings.required_action: Make role behavior explicit: either hide/guard the Classes route for lecturers and redirect them to an allowed page, or use a lecturer-readable class projection while keeping all create/update/enrollment-correction controls admin-only.

findings.evidence: SaveAndPublishAsync publishes ClassLecturerAssigned to the external event bus while a SQL transaction is open. This pattern existed before the enrollment change; rolling back SQL after a successful publish cannot retract the event if commit later fails.
findings.why_it_matters: Catalog and Identity can diverge under a publish-success/commit-failure edge case, but this is pre-existing architecture debt rather than a phase 1-6 regression.
findings.required_action: Track separately and replace the cross-resource transaction illusion with a transactional outbox/idempotent consumer boundary.

build_evidence.backend_production: Fresh Catalog build after remediation: exit 0. The preceding gate also verified all 8 production projects with exit 0; Catalog emitted only the existing NU1504 duplicate-package warning.
build_evidence.backend_solution_context: A full solution build was attempted and failed only in the pre-existing Grading.Api.Tests stub/interface mismatch; tests were intentionally excluded from this cook run.
build_evidence.admin_web: Fresh npm.cmd run build after remediation: exit 0.
build_evidence.user_web: Fresh npm.cmd run build after remediation: exit 0.
